references.title: Incorporating Rich Features into Deep Knowledge Tracing

extracted.Features: [Exercise Tag, Correctedness, First Response Time, Attempt Count, First Action]
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Previous DKT models only consider Question/Skill and correctedness as individual features.
extracted.Novelty of Model: Authors look to improve DKT by adding additional feature sets and an Undercomplete Auto-Encoder to reduce dimensionality and improve training speed.
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Features are represented as a one-hot encoded sparse vector.
extracted.Comments:
Authors add multiple differetn types of feature sets to traditional DKT.

Results from CV show that adding cross-features of skill/correctedness andd time/correctness tend to lead to better performance (both in terms of AUC and R2).

The presence of the Auto-Encoder also improves the performance of the model.
extracted.Limitations:
No model optimization attempts. 

Only focuses on DKT while not considering other viable approaches (at the time- e.g. BKT).

annotations.text: CFA is an important reearch interest, especially in the context of ITS, generating models such as BKT, PFA and DKT (which uses RNN). The problem with the DKT apporach is that it considers only a subset of features related with the knowledge components of a problem.

Thus, the authors’ proposition is 2-fold. First they want to add more features to the problem level. Second, they introduce an Auto-Encoder to reduce training time and resource requirements. Results show that new model obtains higher accuracies than the regular DKT approach.
